content.full_text:
June 22, 1990

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
General Counsel
California Fair Political Practice Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Re: Request for Formal Advice

Dear Mr. Hallabrin:

This letter request advice on behalf of a member of the Larkspur City Council who owns and resides in a home located near a pending project in the City of Larkspur.

Councilman Ronald Arlas owns a home that is located within a radius of 300 feet from the Wareham property. The Wareham property is currently zoned C-1 and C-2 (i.e. general commercial). The Wareham developers have submitted an application for conditional use permit which they are required to obtain for their proposed use in these zones. At the present time, the City of Larkspur is revising its General Plan for the entire city. The draft General Plan which is coming before the Council includes a land use designation change on the Wareham property from general commercial to require a specific plan and mixed land use including residential. If the General Plan is adopted which includes this redesignation of land use on the Wareham property, the application for conditional use permit will probably be subject to denial as being inconsistent with the General Plan.

Therefore, the question becomes: May Councilman Arlas participate in a decision to adopt the new General Plan which includes a possible redesignation of land use on the Wareham property. What would be the appropriate procedure if it is determined that Councilman Arlas has a conflict of interest due to his proximity to the Wareham site?

As a follow-up, since all the Council members live within the City of Larkspur, what procedure should be followed when discussing and voting upon the various elements of the proposed General Plan which would necessarily involve land within 300 feet of their residences?

It is anticipated that the General Plan will come before the

Scott Hallabrin, Esq.
June 22, 1990
Page Two

City Council on July 11, 1990. Therefore, your prompt response to this matter would be greatly appreciated.

Please feel free to contact me if you have any questions or need any further information or materials.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation.

Very truly yours,

[Signature]
RICHARD R. RUDNANSKY

RRR:pm
